and does not fall within the prohibitory clause of section 497(1), Cr.P.C.; therefore, grant of bail
is a rule and refusal an exception. Bail in such offences can only be declined in extraordinary and
exceptional circumstances, which must be specifically shown by the prosecution or complainant.

The literal meaning of “review” is re-examination of something for the sake of correction or improvement. Such transaction would not offend Articles 23 and 24 of the Constitution.

The term \'Jagir\' is shortened form of the Persian word \'Jaygir\' meaning possessing, occupying a place, fixing a habitation, or making a settlement[1].
It denotes an assignment by the sovereign of land revenue of a territory to a Chief or noble, who pledges to support troops, police and other related services. The grant may or may not include a right in the soil that is originally for life but often became hereditary [2].
The jagirdari system essentially was a model of payment by assignments.
